questions.text: A Microsoft Windows is a(n)
questions.solution: Microsoft Windows is the most widely used example of an Operating System, which manages computer hardware and software resources and provides common services for computer programs.

questions.text: OS stands for
questions.solution: OS is the standard abbreviation for Operating System.

questions.text: A co-processor
options: Is relatively easy to support in software
Causes all processor to function equally
Is quite common in modern computer
questions.solution: A co-processor is a supplemental processor that works alongside the primary CPU. While it requires specific drivers or instructions, its integration is often standardized, making the necessary software support relatively straightforward.

questions.text: The ...... program compresses large files into a smaller file
questions.solution: WinZip is a well-known proprietary file archiver and compressor utility used to compress large files.

questions.text: My Computer was introduced from
questions.solution: The "My Computer" icon, central to the desktop interface for accessing drives and folders, was introduced with Microsoft Windows 95.

questions.text: Which of the following is not an operating system?
questions.solution: DOS, Linux, and Windows are operating systems. Oracle is primarily known as a relational database management system (RDBMS).

questions.text: Linux is a(n) ...... operating system
questions.solution: Linux is famously known as a free and open-source operating system.

questions.text: The most recent version of MAC OS is based on the ...... operating system
questions.solution: macOS (formerly Mac OS X) is based on the Unix operating system and utilizes the Darwin core.

questions.text: In Windows, start button is used to
questions.solution: The Start button in Windows provides access to running applications, system settings (Control Panel), and power options like shutting down or restarting the system.

questions.text: The date and time displays on
questions.solution: The system tray (or notification area) is located at the far right of the Windows taskbar, and it typically displays system icons, including the current date and time.

questions.text: ........ Is the layer of a computer system between the hardware and the user?
questions.solution: The Operating System acts as the primary interface layer between the hardware components and the end user or application software.

questions.text: Which is the first program run on a computer when the computer boots up?
questions.solution: After the initial firmware (BIOS/UEFI) runs the bootstrap loader, the operating system kernel is loaded into memory, making it the first major program executed to initiate user interaction.

questions.text: Which of the following requires a device driver?
questions.solution: External devices like a Hard Disk (or disk controller) require a device driver for the operating system to communicate with them and manage their input/output operations.

questions.text: A program in execution is called
questions.solution: In computer science, an instance of a computer program that is being executed is called a process.

questions.text: Which of the following memory unit that processor can access more rapidly
questions.solution: Cache memory is a small, very fast memory buffer located near the CPU that stores frequently used data, providing the fastest access speeds among the given options.

questions.text: Which of the following is an example of a real-time operating system?
questions.solution: Real-Time Operating Systems (RTOS) are essential for systems where timely responses are critical, such as industrial process control systems.

questions.text: Which of the following shutdown method is often called Warm Boot?
questions.solution: A warm boot (or soft boot) is the process of restarting a computer using the operating system's functions (like 'Restart'), without cutting the power completely.

questions.text: If the displayed system time and date are wrong, you can reset it using
questions.solution: The system time and date settings are managed and adjusted via the Date and Time utility found within the Control Panel (or system settings).

questions.text: Which is not application software?
questions.solution: Windows NT is an operating system, which falls under the category of system software. The others (PageMaker, WinWord, Photoshop) are application software.

questions.text: Running multiple programs at the same time is called:
questions.solution: Multitasking is the ability of an operating system to manage and execute multiple processes or applications concurrently.

questions.text: The ...... contains commands associated with the My Computer window
questions.solution: The System menu (often accessible via Alt+Space or the window icon) contains basic window control commands like minimize, maximize, restore, move, and close, which are universally associated with the My Computer window frame.

questions.text: To install the new font in window XP
options: Start -> setting -> control panel -> font -> install new font
Start -> control panel -> font -> install new font
questions.solution: This is the correct procedural path in Windows XP to access the Fonts folder and initiate the installation of a new font file.

questions.text: Which of the following operating system do you choose to implement a client-server network?
questions.solution: Windows 2000 (specifically Windows 2000 Server) was designed as a robust network operating system suitable for implementing and managing client-server architectures, unlike the consumer OS options listed.

questions.text: Which one is not an operating system?
questions.solution: OS/2 and Windows are established operating systems. P11 is not recognized as a standard, general-purpose operating system name.
